heroHeading: Modular Home Extensions, Built for Blackpool
metaTitle: Modular Home Extensions in Blackpool | EazyBase
metaDescription: Factory-built modular extensions for Blackpool homes — kitchens, offices and garden rooms built in Blackburn and installed on site in under a week.
intro:
Anyone who has lived through a winter on the Fylde coast knows what the weather here can do to a building project. Traditional extensions in Blackpool are at the mercy of sea fronts, driving rain and wind that can stall an open building site for weeks. Our approach is different: we build your extension in our Blackburn factory, in a controlled environment, then bring it to your home and install it in under a week.

That is the thinking behind our modular home extensions in Blackpool — a new kitchen, dining room, home office, playroom or garden room, designed around your house and your budget, built in as little as four weeks, and fitted with far less disruption than a conventional build.

From the 1930s semis of Bispham and Marton to former guest houses finding new life as family homes, we design each extension to suit the property it joins — and to stand up to coastal life for the long term.

localAngles.heading: Built for coastal weather
localAngles.body: The single biggest risk to a traditional extension in Blackpool is the weather. An open site near the coast can lose days at a time to wind and rain, and every lost day adds cost and stress. Because your EazyBase extension is manufactured indoors at our Blackburn factory, the build itself is never rained off. We also specify exterior finishes and fixings with salt air in mind, so cladding, glazing and seals are chosen to cope with the conditions a seafront-adjacent home actually faces — not just what works fifty miles inland.

localAngles.heading: Extending a 1930s semi in Bispham or Marton
localAngles.body: Much of Blackpool's housing stock — particularly around Bispham, Marton and Layton — is made up of solid 1930s semis with generous rear gardens. These homes are ideal candidates for a single-storey rear extension: a wider kitchen-diner opening onto the garden, or a proper home office away from the front of the house. We design each module to match the proportions and brickwork character of the original property, so the finished extension feels like it has always belonged rather than something bolted on.

localAngles.heading: A new chapter for former guest houses
localAngles.body: Blackpool has a long tradition of guest houses, and many are now being converted back into spacious family homes. These conversions often leave awkward layouts — small rear kitchens, underused yards — where a well-designed extension can transform how the whole house works. A modular build suits these projects well: the disruptive work happens in our factory, and installation takes under a week, which matters if you are living in the property (or still trading) while the work happens. We can help you plan the extension as part of a wider conversion.

localAngles.heading: Planning permission in Blackpool
localAngles.body: Many single-storey rear extensions fall within permitted development rights, meaning full planning permission is not always required — though you should always confirm your property's position with Blackpool Council before work begins, especially in conservation areas or if the house has been altered before. We handle the drawings and guide you through the process, and because the specification of a factory-built module is fixed and documented from day one, there are no mid-build surprises to complicate approvals or building regulations sign-off.

faqs.q: How long does delivery take from your Blackburn factory to Blackpool?
faqs.a: Our factory is around 30 miles from Blackpool, with a straightforward route along the M55. Your extension is built in the factory in as little as four weeks, then transported to your home and installed on site in under a week. Compared with a traditional build exposed to Fylde coast weather, the whole project is typically faster and far more predictable.

faqs.q: Will I need planning permission for a modular extension in Blackpool?
faqs.a: Not always. Permitted development rights often cover single-storey rear extensions within certain size limits, but every property is different — always check with Blackpool Council, particularly if your home is in a conservation area or has been extended before. We prepare the drawings and support you through planning and building regulations from the start.

faqs.q: Can you install on a typical Blackpool street with limited parking and access?
faqs.a: In most cases, yes. Before we build anything, we carry out a site survey covering access, parking and crane or delivery positioning — including the narrower streets and rear alleys common in parts of Blackpool. We plan the installation day carefully with you, and because fitting takes under a week, any disruption to neighbours and parking is short-lived.

faqs.q: What do Blackpool homeowners typically use a modular extension for?
faqs.a: The most popular projects locally are open-plan kitchen-diners on 1930s semis in areas like Bispham and Marton, garden rooms and home offices for people working from home, and kids' playrooms. We also work with owners converting former guest houses into family homes, where an extension can resolve the awkward layouts those buildings often leave behind.
